summary: A Ukrainian esports player with the call sign "Tucha" transitioned to become an SVO UAV operator after nine years of gaming experience, joining the "Burevestnik" unmanned systems regiment and quickly adapting to her new role. In the Kharkiv region, Ukrainian forces destroyed a Polish AHS Krab 155-mm self-propelled howitzer using an optical fiber FPV drone called "Provod." Ukrainian interceptor drones have emerged as a key element of short-range air defense, with over 300 deployed in the past year to counter Russian Geran drones, according to Ukrainian Armed Forces Commander-in-Chief Syrskyi. Meanwhile, Ukrainian experts are set to depart for the Middle East to train Gulf countries in countering drones, following interest expressed by several Middle Eastern nations. On the battlefield, Russian soldiers were hit by two Ukrainian FPV drones simultaneously while hiding behind a tree, and the "Rubikon" Center reported 88 targets destroyed on March 7, 2026, in the Donbas and Krasny Liman directions.
